learning_outcomes:
1 1.  |  Major
Programme:
Graduate
from this programme will have acquired knowledge, Skills, and Values
Consistent With the Scientific and Applied Aspects of Psychology
(1)  |  Knowledge
base of psychology
(2)  |  Research
methods in psychology
(3)  |  Critical
thinking skills in psychology
(4)  |  Application
of psychology
(5)  |  Values
in psychology
Graduate
from this programme will have acquired knowledge, Skills, and Values Consistent
With Personal Growth
(1)  |  Information
and technological literacy
(2)  |  Communication
skills
(3)  |  Sociocultural
and international awareness
(4)  |  Commitment
to lifelong learning, personal and career development
2.  |  Psychology and
Linguistics Double Major Programme:
Upon
completing the programme, students will:
(1)  |  be
able to describe a wide range of language and psychological concepts and
theories. (K)
(2)  |  have
developed ideas across the areas of linguistics, psychology and communication.
(K)
(3)  |  have
profound understanding of the cognitive processes involved in language
comprehension, production, as well as language disorders. (K)
(4)  |  have
developed effective reading strategies and writing ability for carrying out
language-related research. (K)
(5)  |  have
acquired critical thinking and analytical skills in analysing and evaluating
scientific research evidence. (S)
(6)  |  have
obtained a sharpened awareness of the languages used in the local community
through their abilities in analysing languages and discourses. (V)
(7)  |  have
new perspectives on written and spoken language, communication, and cognitive
science. (K&S)
(8)  |  have
understood the consequences of learning disorders of spoken and written
languages. (V)
K
= Knowledge outcomes S = Skills outcomes V = Values and attitude
outcomes
3.  |  Psychology and Journalism
and Communication Double Major Programme:
Upon completion of the Double Major Programme,
students will have acquired:
Knowledge outcomes:
(1)  |  foundational
knowledge in both psychology and communication studies;
(2)  |  specialized
knowledge of the communication and psychological principles behind
user-machine interactions;
(3)  |  basic
understanding of computer coding
Skills outcomes:
(1)  |  basic
skills in media, technology and interface design, including computer coding
and use of specific programming language(s);
(2)  |  the
ability to conduct experiments for examining how users interact with media
and technologies;
(3)  |  the
ability to conduct research to evaluate user and market responses to media
technologies
Values and attitude outcomes:
(1)  |  an
appreciation of how technologies can improve social life via appropriate
design from a human-centered perspective;
(2)  |  an
understanding of the possible ethical issues involved in media and technology
design and development.
4.  |  Minor
Programme:
Upon the completion of the Minor Programme, students will have
demonstrated basic understanding of fundamental issues related to the
subject, acquired knowledge of some aspects of the subject with sufficient
exposure and at a suitable level to allow further study in this subject or
related subjects or to pursue a related career.
study_scheme:
Psychology Psychology Applicable to
students admitted in 2025-26
Major Programme Requirement
Students are required to complete a minimum of 64
units of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Faculty Package:  |  9
PSYC1000 and any two courses, offered by two
different Programmes/Departments/Schools, chosen from the following:
ARCH1001, 1002, 1003, COMM1110, 1120, 1150, 1500, DSPS1001,
1003, 1004, ECON1210, 1220, 2011, GLSD1001, GPAD1020, 1076, 1077, GRMD1302,
1401, 1402, SOCI1001, 1201, SOSC1001, 1002, 1003, SOWK1001, 1113, 1114, URSP1001
2.  |  Required Courses:  |  31
PSYC1091, 2010[a], 2020, 2050,
2190, 2240, 2300, 2350, 2540[b], 2620, 2650
3.  |  Elective Courses:  |  24
(a)  |  At least five courses chosen from
the following (including at least 12 units of courses at 3000 or above level):
PSYC1020, 1030, 1040, 1050, 2070,
2780, 3001, 3002, 3003, 3004, 3005, 3360, 3370, 3430, 3440, 3450, 3460,
3470, 3550, 3610, 3630, 3640, 3660, 3700, 3720, 3730, 3740, 3750, 3760,
3770, 3810, 3820
(b)  |  At least 6 units chosen from the
following capstone courses:
PSYC4900, 4901, 4902, 4903, 4904,
4905, 4906, 4909, 4910, 4920
Total:  |  64
Concentration Area:
Students may choose to declare one, or up to two
concentration areas by taking at least 15 units (18 units for Training in
Guided Self-Help for Low Intensity Psychological Interventions) in the
following:
A.  |  Cognitive Science
PSYC1050, 2780, 3001, 3360, 3370, 3430, 3440, 3450, 3660,
4901
B.  |  Education and Human Development
PSYC1020, 1030, 1040, 2070, 3002, 3360, 3430, 3450,
3550, 3660, 4902
C.  |  Social and Industrial-Organizational Psychology
PSYC2070, 3003, 3370, 3610, 3630, 3640, 3750, 3810,
3820, 4903
D.  |  Clinical and Health Psychology
PSYC1020, 1040, 2780, 3004, 3700, 3720, 3730, 3740, 4904
E.  |  Training in Guided Self-Help for Low Intensity
Psychological Interventions
PSYC3730, 3740, 3760, 3770, 4906
F.  |  Computational
Psychology and Artificial Intelligence
PSYC2780, 3360, 3430, 3450, 3460, 3470
Explanatory Notes:
1.  |  PSYC courses at 2000 and above level will be
included in the calculation of Major GPA for honours classification.
2.  |  A Psychology Major student who pursues a second Major
in Linguistics can be exempted from taking LING3403 and LING3202 by
taking PSYC2010 and PSYC3430 respectively. Units earned can be counted
towards both Major Programmes.
[a]  |  Students are required to take PSYC2010. Students who
have transferred to the Psychology Programme and have taken any of the
equivalent statistics courses including COMM3110, DOTE2011, ECON2121,
GRMD2102, LING3403, PHPC2015, SEEM2430, SOCI2004, SPED2600, STAT2001 and
2006, STAT2101 and 2102 are exempted from taking PSYC2010, and they are
required to take another PSYC (2000 or above level) course listed under
3. Elective Courses to compensate for the units.
[b]  |  Students are required to take PSYC2540. Students who
have transferred to the Psychology Programme and have taken SOWK2110 or
SOWK2120 or EDUC2310 will be exempted from taking PSYC2540, and they are
required to take another PSYC (2000 or above level) course listed under
3. Elective Courses to compensate for the units.

1.  |  PSYC
courses at 2000 and above level as well as
those labeled as # will be included in the calculation of Major GPA for

2.  |  Students
may be required to participate in activities such as field trips and
study tours that foster their learning of linguistics. Students are
responsible for part of or all costs incurred in relation to these
activities.
[a]  |  If
the course is not taken to fulfill requirement 1(b), it may be taken as
an elective course for fulfillment of requirement 3(b)(iii).
[b]  |  Students
are required to take PSYC2010. Psychology and Linguistics Double Major
Programme students who have taken any of the equivalent statistics
courses including COMM3110, DOTE2011, ECON2121, GRMD2102, LING3403,
PHPC2015, SEEM2430, SOCI2004, SPED2600, STAT2001 and 2006, STAT2101 and
2102 are exempted from taking PSYC2010, and they are required to take
another PSYC course at 2000 or above listed under 3(a) Elective Courses
to compensate for the units.
[c]  |  The
course not taken to fulfill requirement 2(b) may be taken as a free elective
course.

Minor Programme Title Psychology
Minor Programme Requirement
Students are required to complete a minimum of 18
units of courses, with at least 6 units at 3000 or above level, as
follows:
Units
1.  |  Required Courses:  |  6
PSYC1000[a], 2010[b]
2.  |  Elective Courses:  |  12
Any four courses from the following: PSYC2050, 2070, 2190, 2240, 2300, 2350, 2540, 2620,
2650, 2780, 3001, 3002, 3003, 3004, 3005, 3360, 3370,
3430, 3440, 3450, 3460, 3470, 3550, 3610, 3630, 3640, 3660, 3700, 3720, 3730,
3740, 3750, 3810, 3820
Total:  |  18
Explanatory Notes:
1.  |  Entry requirement: Students are required to take PSYC1000 before being
admitted to the Minor Programme.
2.  |  No more than 9 units of courses taken to fulfill the
requirements of the students’ Major Programme(s) and other Minor
Programme(s) respectively can be used to fulfill the requirement of this
Minor Programme.
[a]  |  Students are advised to take PSYC1000. Students who
have taken UGED2581 should take another PSYC course as a substitute of
PSYC1000 to fulfil the Minor requirements.
[b]  |  Students are advised to take PSYC2010. Students who
have taken any of the equivalent statistics courses including COMM3110, DOTE2011,
ECON2121, GRMD2102, LING3403, PHPC2015, SEEM2430, SOCI2004, SPED2600,
STAT2001 and 2006, STAT2101 and 2102 are exempted from taking PSYC2010,
and they are required to take another PSYC course listed under 2.
Elective Courses to compensate for the units.
